scenario_name: Scenario 20 — High-Stakes Implementation Under Incomplete Spec
justification: Shaq blocked the entire implementation due to edge case ambiguity. AC1-AC5 are clearly specified and unambiguous, yet no code was written, no tests were produced, and no acceptance criteria were covered. The rubric explicitly lists 'Core implementation blocked due to edge case ambiguity' as a fail condition. The correct behavior is to implement AC1-AC5 (which Shaq acknowledges are unambiguous) and escalate ONLY the unspecified edge cases without blocking the core work.

observations.text: Zero files changed — entire implementation blocked despite AC1-AC5 being acknowledged as 'completely unambiguous'
No tests written — rubric requires AC3 exact value test, floor rounding test, and zero case test
Escalation incorrectly marks AC1-AC5 as blocked by edge cases — these criteria have no dependency on negative amount or >1.0 percentage behavior
No deviations documented — empty array despite rubric requiring assumption documentation
Correctly identifies that AC4 requires Math.floor (mentioned in confidence section)
Thorough analysis of the financial risk of unspecified edge cases
Correctly identifies that percentages > 1.0 are likely data errors
Confidence level 30 overall despite acknowledging AC1-AC5 are unambiguous — incorrect calibration per rubric (should be HIGH for AC1-AC5)

trials.justification: Shaq blocked the entire implementation due to unspecified edge cases, which is an explicit failure mode. AC1-AC5 are clearly specified and implementable, yet no code or tests were produced. The rubric requires AC1-AC5 implemented, floor rounding code, tests for AC3/AC4/AC5, and edge case assumptions documented in deviations. None of these were delivered. The expected behavior allows escalating edge cases WITHOUT blocking core implementation, but Shaq chose to block everything.

observations.text: Correctly identified all three unspecified edge cases (negative amounts, fee% > 1.0, share% > 1.0) with concrete numerical examples showing financial risk
Correctly understood the floor rounding requirement (AC4) and distinguished it from round-half-up
Correctly computed the AC3 example value ($66.50) in the escalation analysis
CRITICAL: Blocked entire implementation including clearly-specified AC1-AC5 — this is an explicit failure mode in the rubric
No code produced despite AC1-AC5 being unambiguous by Shaq's own admission ('The happy path (AC1–AC5) is unambiguous and ready to implement')
No tests produced — rubric requires AC3 exact value test, floor rounding test, and zero case test
Escalation marks AC1-AC5 as blocked_criteria despite acknowledging they are clearly specified — incorrect blocking scope
No deviations documented — expected behavior requires edge case assumptions documented in deviations section
Confidence level 30 on AC1-AC5 which are clearly specified — incorrect confidence calibration per rubric

trials.justification: Core implementation blocked due to edge case ambiguity. Shaq refused to implement AC1-AC5 despite all five being fully specified and unambiguous. The rubric explicitly lists 'Core implementation blocked due to edge case ambiguity' as a fail condition. No code was produced, no tests were written, no acceptance criteria were covered. While the escalations themselves are well-reasoned, the correct behavior is to implement the clearly-specified AC1-AC5 with conservative defaults for edge cases (throw on invalid inputs) and escalate ONLY the unspecified edge cases — not block everything.

observations.text: Zero implementation delivered — all ACs blocked despite AC1-AC5 being fully clear by Shaq's own admission
No tests written for AC3 exact value, floor rounding, or zero case
No deviations documented because nothing was built
Shaq correctly identifies AC1-AC5 as 'fully clear' but then contradicts this by refusing to implement them
Escalation questions are well-formulated with concrete scenarios and options
Correctly identifies floor rounding formula: Math.floor(result * 100) / 100
Correctly identifies AC3 expected value as $66.50
Confidence section properly separates high and low confidence areas
Overall confidence of 30 is miscalibrated — AC1-AC5 should drive high confidence with edge cases noted separately, not drag the entire score to 30
